response.justification: Bogdan is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented engineer with genuine high-throughput experience (microservices handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic), strong TypeScript, and directly relevant AI-native exposure through MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling at re:cinq. However, the role's must-have of strong Python is entirely absent from his resume, his deepest distributed-systems work was in DLang and ended in 2020, and there is little concrete evidence of directing technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder management, or operational on-call ownership at Staff scope. He is worth a conversation if Python can be validated or waived, but he does not clearly meet the bar on paper.
strengths: Current hands-on work on AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflow frameworks — matches the role's AI-native baseline
Real high-throughput, low-latency systems experience: async I/O microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Strong TypeScript/Node depth plus broad polyglot range and founder-level ownership (GISCollective, Kubernetes-deployed platform)
concerns: No Python anywhere on the resume — a stated must-have for the role
Distributed-systems depth is dated (2018–2020) and built in DLang, a niche stack with limited transfer signal
Limited evidence of Staff-scope technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder management, or formal on-call/incident-response experience
